name: Ashi
description: Ashi, also known as Arshtat, is a prominent Yazata (divine being) in Zoroastrianism, embodying wealth, prosperity, and righteousness. She is a powerful figure associated with material and spiritual abundance, representing the blessings bestowed upon those who follow the path of Asha, the divine order. Ashi is not merely a deity of riches, but also a moral force, rewarding virtuous behavior and piety. Her name itself signifies 'righteousness' or 'truth.' In Zoroastrian cosmology, Ashi is a vital ally to the righteous, offering protection and guidance against the forces of evil and deprivation. She is often invoked in prayers and rituals aimed at securing blessings, good fortune, and the fulfillment of one's righteous aspirations. Her association with fertility and well-being further underscores her importance in the Zoroastrian worldview, making her a significant figure in both personal and communal life. Ashi's influence extends beyond material gain, encompassing spiritual growth and the attainment of inner peace through adherence to the principles of Asha.
subtitle: Goddess of Fortune and Righteousness

keyMyths.title: Ashi and the Righteous Man
keyMyths.description: The myth recounts a pious man who, despite facing hardship, remained steadfast in his devotion to Ahura Mazda and upheld the principles of Asha. Ashi, witnessing his unwavering faith and commitment to righteousness, descended from the heavens and bestowed upon him immense wealth and prosperity. This tale serves as a moral lesson, highlighting the importance of piety and the rewards awaiting those who embrace the path of truth and righteousness. It demonstrates Ashi's role as a divine benefactor who favors the virtuous and grants them material and spiritual blessings.

keyMyths.title: Ashi's Battle Against Angra Mainyu
keyMyths.description: Ashi is depicted as a valiant warrior who actively combats Angra Mainyu, the embodiment of evil and chaos, and his malevolent forces. She protects the faithful from the temptations of greed and corruption, ensuring that the pursuit of wealth does not lead to moral decay. This myth underscores Ashi's role as a guardian of righteousness, who actively fights against the forces that threaten the divine order and the well-being of humanity. Her victory ensures the continued prosperity and spiritual growth of those who follow the path of Asha.

keyMyths.title: The Invocation of Ashi for Blessings
keyMyths.description: Rituals are performed to invoke Ashi's blessings, seeking her favor for wealth, prosperity, and good fortune. These ceremonies often involve offerings and prayers, expressing gratitude for her benevolence and appealing for continued guidance and protection. The invocation of Ashi reinforces the importance of seeking divine favor through righteous actions and devotion, highlighting the reciprocal relationship between humans and the divine in Zoroastrianism. By honoring Ashi, individuals hope to align themselves with the principles of Asha and receive her bountiful gifts.
